item.title: Senegal Bonds Go From Emerging-Market Leaders to Laggards as Default Fears Rise
item.description: Senegal’s dollar bonds traded near record lows Tuesday as a deepening political crisis in the West African nation turned a top emerging-markets performer into the worst.

item.title: How Lawsuits Alleging Social Media Harms Threaten Big Tech
item.description: A flood of lawsuits filed in recent years against social media giants claim that by engineering their platforms to be hard to resist, the companies are causing serious harm to young users. In the first case to go to trial, a jury in March found Meta Platforms Inc. and Google negligent in the design and operation of their platforms — a landmark verdict.

item.title: UniCredit Says Commerzbank Offer Set to Push Direct Stake to 34%
item.description: UniCredit SpA’s €38.6 billion ($45 billion) takeover offer for Commerzbank AG has been accepted by enough investors to push the stake over 30% once it takes ownership of the shares.

item.title: Russia Gushes Crude Exports as Drone Strikes Hobble Refineries
item.description: Year-to-date flows exceed averages for each year since Moscow’s troops invaded Ukraine in 2022

item.title: Barclays Pushes UK to Be More Welcoming to Foreign Investment
item.description: Barclays Plc urged the UK to do more to attract a broader range of foreign investment and help add trillions of pounds more capital from overseas.

item.title: Virgin Galactic’s 200% Rally Burns Shorts as Retail Rushes In
item.description: A rapid rally in shares of Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc. has burned short sellers betting against the space tourism firm, according to data from S3 Partners.

item.title: BofA Sees Strong South Africa Deal Market Despite Global Turmoil
item.description: Bank of America Corp. expects strong deal-making activity in South Africa to continue in this year despite ongoing global volatility, the head of its local operation said.

item.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Model Access to 150 Additional Groups
item.description: Anthropic PBC is allowing 150 additional organizations around the world to access Mythos, an artificial intelligence model aimed at finding cybersecurity vulnerabilities that the company has said was too dangerous to make available to the general public.

item.title: AI Voice Startup ElevenLabs Targets Warsaw Hub Expansion in Push for CEE Clients
item.description: ElevenLabs sees Warsaw as its fastest-growing hub as the artificial intelligence voice company chases new customers in central and eastern Europe.

item.title: T-Mobile Wants to Sell a Lifestyle, Not Just Wireless
item.description: Felisha White, a 34-year-old T-Mobile US Inc. customer in New York, stays up late on Monday nights to see what the latest deal drops will be from her wireless provider.

item.title: Albania PM Vows to Press Ahead With Kushner Resort Amid Protests
item.description: Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama vowed to press ahead with a €4 billion ($4.7 billion) luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump, which has sparked mounting protests.

item.title: South Africa Beats Primary Budget Surplus Goal, Stabilizes Debt
item.description: South Africa posted a third consecutive primary budget surplus, underscoring the government’s commitment to repairing state finances.

item.title: BOE Asset Sales Drive Up UK Borrowing Costs, Staff Paper Finds
item.description: Bank of England asset sales have driven up UK borrowing costs by almost half a percentage point, adding to the pressure on Britain’s stretched government finances.

item.title: Hammack Says Fed May Need to Act Soon Against High Inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said it’s reasonable to hold interest rates steady for now given uncertainties about the economic outlook, but officials may need to act soon to address elevated inflation.

item.title: Palo Alto CEO’s Pay, Now Nearly $100 Million, Is Most Rejected in Corporate America
item.description: A majority of Palo Alto Networks investors have voted against the cybersecurity company’s executive pay seven times in 11 years. That’s despite the company’s stock soaring.

item.title: US Stock Futures Pull Back as Traders Evaluate Mideast News
item.description: US stocks declined before the bell on Tuesday as traders parsed the latest developments in the Middle East.

item.title: China Says UK Ties ‘Returning to Normal’ After Years of Tension
item.description: Britain should set out a path for more Chinese investment as relations between the two countries normalize, Foreign Minister Wang Yi said while welcoming his counterpart Yvette Cooper.

item.title: Sheinbaum Leans on Private Investors to Fix Blackout-Prone Grid
item.description: Investment in Mexico’s struggling electricity sector is roaring to life as a slew of deals in power plants, renewables and grid upgrades pave the way for President Claudia Sheinbaum’s $56 billion energy plan.

item.title: Startup Impulse Space Raises $500 Million, Valued at $4 Billion
item.description: Impulse Space, a startup backed by billionaire Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund, raised $500 million in a new funding round, the company announced.

item.title: Withings Debuts New Smart Scale Marketed Toward GLP-1 Users
item.description: Withings, the French company that pioneered the category of Wi-Fi bathroom scales, unveiled a new model with advanced body composition scanning abilities that is being marketed toward people on GLP-1 weight loss drugs.

item.title: Wall Street Analysts Turn Skeptical After Two-Month Stocks Rally
item.description: US stocks may be vaulting from one record to the next, but Wall Street analysts covering them are in no rush to keep up.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 2, 2026
item.description: A rally in US stocks is stalling, with S&P 500 Index futures down 0.2% at 7:46 a.m. in New York.

item.title: Digital Bank Forbright Seeking to Raise $158 Million in US IPO
item.description: Forbright Inc., a financial services platform that focuses on middle-market lending and digital consumer banking, is seeking to raise as much as $158 million in an US initial public offering.

item.title: EM Currencies Advance on Iran Talks With Rand Leading Gains
item.description: Most emerging market currencies advanced outside Asia as hopes of a deal in the Middle East drove down oil prices and boosted risk sentiment.

item.title: SNB Has No Exchange Rate Target for Franc, Schlegel Says
item.description: The Swiss National Bank doesn’t target a specific exchange rate, President Martin Schlegel said, repeating the central bank’s position that it has a greater willingness to intervene in the franc.

item.title: Walmart’s Answer to Apple Pay Wants to Be Your Favorite Financial App
item.description: Backed by the retailer and Ribbit Capital, OnePay is reaching beyond its initial customer base to take on banks.

item.title: Persian Gulf LNG Exporters Are Adopting Shadow-Fleet Tactics
item.description: QatarEnergy and Adnoc are using methods typically employed by Russia to get their gas across the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Swiss 10-Million Cap Damage Can’t Be Easily Dodged, Lombard Says
item.description: The strict limits Switzerland must implement in a proposal to cap its population at 10 million would make economic fallout hard to avoid, according to Lombard Odier.

item.title: Nvidia Is Bigger, Stronger, and Trying to Slay the Laptop Dragon Again
item.description: In its biggest new product for consumers in years, Nvidia is now making PC chips

item.title: Trump Lowers Some US Tariffs in Bid to ‘Spur’ Investment
item.description: The import tax on farm and construction machinery is going down to 15%
